_note: Provenance + licence registry for the pharmacology clinical DATA sources (FL-30 Step 3). Distinct from integration/harvest-manifest.json, which tracks harvested CODE repos. Every clinical fact authored into mcp/servers/pharmacology/data/* MUST cite one of these source ids in its ProvenanceSchema.source_ref. Sources with use_restriction='structure_only' (STOPP/START, TDM, DrugBank, AusDI, APF22) supply FACTS + STRUCTURE + CITATION only — no monograph/criteria prose or tables are copied into the datastore (FL-30 Guardrail 1). Facts are not copyrightable; the source is cited as where the fact was found. This file is the backbone of the provenance-coverage report.

sources.name: PBS Public API v3 (Pharmaceutical Benefits Scheme)
sources.provider: Australian Government Department of Health and Aged Care
sources.notes: Cached monthly (API design is download-and-store; rate-limited ~1 req/20s, current + 12 months). Subscription key via the secrets seam. Does NOT provide interactions / NTI / renal dosing / allergy / SUSMP scheduling / PDMP.

sources.name: Standard for the Uniform Scheduling of Medicines and Poisons (SUSMP / Poisons Standard)
sources.notes: Legally-binding instrument on the Federal Register of Legislation. Authoritative for AU scheduling + the S8 firewall gate. NB: PBS 'schedule codes' are a DIFFERENT concept (subsidy listing), not SUSMP poisons schedules.

sources.name: RxNorm (US National Library of Medicine)
sources.notes: Country-agnostic generic-level drug identity/normalisation (most generics common to US/UK/AU).

sources.name: WHO ATC/DDD classification
sources.provider: WHO Collaborating Centre for Drug Statistics Methodology

sources.name: STOPP/START criteria version 3 (O'Mahony et al., Age and Ageing 2023)
sources.provider: O'Mahony et al. / Oxford University Press
sources.notes: STRUCTURE + THRESHOLD FACTS + CITATION only. Criterion prose NOT copied into the datastore; encoded rules are original authoring corroborated against AMH/TGA. Guardrail 1.

sources.name: Therapeutic Drug Monitoring reference (NHS Tayside / Ninewells formulary)
sources.provider: Department of Biochemical Medicine, Ninewells Hospital
sources.notes: STRUCTURE + reference FACTS (therapeutic intervals, sampling timing, steady-state) + CITATION only. Intervals corroborated against AMH; prose/tables not copied. Guardrail 1.

sources.name: DrugBank Narrow Therapeutic Index category (DBCAT003972)
sources.notes: STRUCTURE-ONLY POINTER. DrugBank content is proprietary (the page 403s to automated fetch). No list, text, or values captured; the NTI list is authored from open sources (TDM + primary literature) + clinician authoring. Guardrail 1.

sources.name: AusDI (structure/UX scoping only)
sources.notes: STRUCTURE-ONLY, one read-only session (clinician-driven, FL-30 Step 3b). Field taxonomy / record decomposition observed and recorded in docs/structure-notes/ausdi-structure-observations.md as 'structure reference — not content'. NO page text, tables, or record values persisted. Guardrail 1.

sources.name: Australian Pharmaceutical Formulary and Handbook, 22nd edn (APF22)
sources.notes: AUTHORITATIVE reference work, attested by the clinician (KL, 2026-07-14) as a trusted source for FACTUAL use. Used for FACTS + STRUCTURE + CITATION ONLY — e.g. dosing-range facts, TDM target/threshold facts (Section B Table B.2), AU pregnancy-category facts, crush/modification-of-oral-formulation facts, urine/faeces discolouration facts, and the Section D monograph heading taxonomy. Facts are not copyrightable and are cited to APF22 as their source; APF22 PROSE and COMPILED TABLES are NOT reproduced into the datastore (Guardrail 1). A PSA CONTENT LICENCE is NOT held — reproducing APF expression/tables (beyond facts) would require that licence and is an org/legal decision, not covered by this attestation. Where a public primary source is the better origin (RASML/TGA for advisory labels, TGA Prescribing Medicines in Pregnancy for categories), prefer it and cite APF22 only corroboratively.

sources.name: Required Advisory Statements for Medicine Labels (RASML)
sources.notes: The authoritative AU PRIMARY source for warning_labels (source_scheme:'RASML'). Public legal instrument — required advisory statements may be used. Preferred over APF's compiled CAL assignments (© PSA).

sources.name: TGA Prescribing Medicines in Pregnancy database
sources.notes: Authoritative AU PRIMARY source for pregnancy_risk (TGA categories A/B1/B2/B3/C/D/X). Categories are public facts. The long tail should be BULK-synced from this database (like pbs), not hand-authored, to avoid category-assignment errors; the current seed is a confident safety-critical subset only.

sources.name: TGA Product Information (PI) documents
sources.notes: The AU PRIMARY regulatory dose source, sibling of tga-pregnancy and rasml-tga. PI is public and sponsor-authoritative; dose FACTS are citable to the PI document id + version + retrieval date (facts are not copyrightable). PROSE IS NOT REPRODUCED. NOT YET CONNECTED - access to the TGA PI source is an OPERATOR input, the SAME input FL-05's pregnancy-risk-bulk-sync-pending waits on. licence_status stays 'documented' (not 'verified') until that access lands and the document terms are confirmed on-source. Consumed only by the tga_pi origin channel of DoseGuidanceSchema (C4); no record cites it yet.

sources.name: Amass RegulatoryCore (unified FDA + EMA authorisations)
sources.provider: Amass (aggregator of FDA SPL / EMA SmPC + EPAR)
sources.licence: Aggregated public regulatory documents (FDA SPL: US public domain; EMA SmPC: (c) EMA)
sources.notes: *** NOT AN AUSTRALIAN SOURCE. NEVER AN ORIGIN FOR AN AU DOSE. *** Probed live 2026-07-15: the agency enum is exactly [FDA, EMA] - there is no TGA in it. ROLE CORRECTED 2026-07-15 (operator ruling): AMASS is NOT a verification oracle collapsed to pass/fail. It is real regulatory knowledge and is stored as what it IS - US dose guidance / EU dose guidance - in the parallel ENGINE-ISOLATED `international_dose_guidance` register (no accessor; a foreign label cannot become an AU dose by construction, exactly as dose_evidence is isolated). The AU dose record's `au_congruence` block then APPRAISES the AU dose against those labels: congruent | non_congruent | no_comparator. **The appraisal ANNOTATES; it NEVER vetoes.** A non-congruent AU dose SHIPS, carrying the appraisal, and the clinician sees the AU dose and the foreign label side by side (Guardrail 2: the engine proposes, a registered practitioner disposes). This replaces the original design, which omitted a 'diverges' state so a differing AU dose could not be written. That was wrong twice over: it INVERTED the jurisdiction rule (an AU dose's authority is APF22/TGA PI - a US/EU regulator has no standing to veto it, and letting it do so makes AU subordinate to a foreign agency), and it conflated 'different jurisdiction' with 'wrong' (AU/US/EU labels legitimately differ by approved indication, population and regulatory history - divergence is the NORMAL case, so binning on it was over-triage). Licence: label FACTS + citation (amass_id, source_url) only. FDA SPL is US public domain; EMA SmPC is (c) EMA - so no SPL/SmPC prose is copied, on the same facts-and-citation basis that governs apf22. Reached at AUTHORING time from scripts/ tooling (the dose-evidence get_article_metadata precedent) - NOT a runtime dependency, no receipt-mode impact.

sources.name: MIMS Web Services (Australia)
sources.notes: Phase D SCAFFOLD, NOT CONNECTED (2026-07-24; ADR docs/structure-notes/vendor-procurement-adr.md). Candidate commercial drug-knowledge feed for the LicensedFeedSource / cds-adapter — the patient-facing authoritative dose/interaction/contraindication source, EMPTY->HARD_FAIL today. REST, AU-hosted; MIMS CDS modules map ~1:1 onto Trunk 8.0 firewall checks (DDI, allergy, contraindication, duplicate therapy, pregnancy, lactation); editorially independent of manufacturer PI. use_restriction:structure_only because the feed is QUERIED LIVE under licence — no MIMS content is copied into the repo datastore. licence_status:pending — no contract/credentials/licence clearance held (operator + counsel). No fact in data/* cites this id yet. Connecting it is build-order #1 / FL-34, DEFERRED. AMT gives identity, not decision support, which is why a drug-data buy is the floor.

sources.name: Parchment (parchment.health) — AU eScript + integration API
sources.notes: Phase D SCAFFOLD, NOT CONNECTED (2026-07-24; ADR). PRIMARY make-or-buy evaluation target (blueprint C-4): an ADHA-approved AU integration API CLAIMING to bundle eRx conformance + HI Services (identity) + SafeScript/QScript + all state RTPM + the MIMS medicine database (subscription covered). If it holds, ONE contract covers pharmacology + identity-au + messaging-geo. *** CLAIM UNVERIFIED — vendor claim, NOT a fact; operator to verify independently against Parchment's contract/API terms. *** Ships an MCP server, kept OUT of the harvest-manifest (evaluation only, not wrapped, per operator decision). MIMS-direct is the documented fallback. licence_status:pending; use_restriction:structure_only (live-queried, not ingested). Connecting it is build-order #1 / FL-34, DEFERRED; dispensing-lawfulness + no-revenue-share are counsel-owned (blueprint C-6).
